pr.title: Add Windows build job and improve speed of Ubuntu build
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added a Windows GitHub Actions build alongside a renamed Ubuntu build, reduced Ubuntu CI setup time by downloading a pinned WiBo release instead of building WiBo from source, switched to a smaller MELEE_COMPILERS.zip containing only needed compiler binaries including powerpc-eabi-as.exe and a pre-patched 1.1 linker, and made small Makefile changes so builds work under Windows shells.
what_changed: Renamed the original GitHub Actions job from build to build-ubuntu and pinned it to ubuntu-22.04 plus devkitpro/devkitppc:20230110 instead of using latest tags.
Added a build-windows GitHub Actions job running on windows-2022 with the same GENERATE_MAP=1 and NON_MATCHING=1 matrix as Ubuntu.
Windows CI downloads MELEE_COMPILERS.zip with PowerShell, expands it under a temporary directory, copies the compiler payload into tools\mwcc_compiler, and builds with make WINDOWS=1.
Windows CI explicitly passes AS=".\tools\mwcc_compiler\powerpc-eabi-as.exe" and uses -j "$env:NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS".
Ubuntu CI no longer checks out and builds the WiBo repository; it downloads the WiBo 0.4.1 binary, verifies a hard-coded sha1, marks it executable, and uses WINE=./tools/wibo.
Ubuntu CI changed from installing multilib/i386 build dependencies manually to using awalsh128/cache-apt-pkgs-action@v1.2.4 for build-essential and libc6-dev.

Compiler download changed from GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip with local mwld patching to MELEE_COMPILERS.zip, which the PR body says contains only required binaries, a pre-patched 1.1 linker, and powerpc-eabi-as.exe for Windows.
actions/upload-artifact was updated from v2 to v3.
Calc progress command formatting was expanded into multi-line shell blocks for readability.
Makefile changed HOSTCC from cc to gcc.
Makefile replaced shell redirection `echo $(O_FILES) > build/o_files` with GNU make's `$(file >build/o_files, $(O_FILES))` so the object list file can be generated under Windows shells.
smart_moves: Pinned OS/container/action/tool versions instead of relying on latest, reducing the chance of future CI breakage from upstream image or action changes.
Downloaded a known WiBo 0.4.1 binary and verified it with sha1, trading slower source builds for a faster but still integrity-checked CI setup.
Used one compiler archive tailored for this project, avoiding unnecessary Wii compiler payloads and removing the previous CI-time linker patch step.
Mirrored the Ubuntu matrix on Windows so both matching-map and NON_MATCHING configurations are checked across platforms.
Used Makefile's built-in `file` function to generate the linker object list, avoiding shell-specific echo/redirection behavior.

Kept map upload on Ubuntu while still running progress calculation on Windows for the GENERATE_MAP=1 case, based on the diff and PR body statement that Windows mirrors Ubuntu except for map artifact upload.
decomp_lessons: Cross-platform decomp CI should avoid assuming POSIX shell semantics in Makefile recipes when the workflow is expected to run under Windows shells.
For generated response files such as linker object lists, GNU make built-ins can be more portable than shell redirection.
When CI depends on binary tools such as WiBo or proprietary compiler bundles, pin versions and verify downloaded artifacts where possible.
Adding Windows CI may require explicitly selecting assembler paths and passing a WINDOWS=1 Makefile flag rather than relying on Unix-style tool discovery.
Avoid `latest` in GitHub Actions images, containers, and external tool setup when deterministic builds matter.
A project-specific compiler archive can simplify CI and reduce setup time, but the evidence here only documents its use via Discord-hosted MELEE_COMPILERS.zip and the PR author's explanation.
naming_conventions: GitHub Actions jobs were named by platform: `build-ubuntu` and `build-windows`.
Workflow matrix variable remained `makeflags` with values `GENERATE_MAP=1` and `NON_MATCHING=1`.
The Windows build used the Makefile flag `WINDOWS=1`.
The WiBo binary was placed at `tools/wibo` for Ubuntu CI.
The compiler bundle was copied or moved into `tools/mwcc_compiler` on both platforms.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The CI matrix continued to test both `GENERATE_MAP=1` and `NON_MATCHING=1`, preserving coverage for map generation/progress reporting and non-matching build configuration.
Windows CI explicitly used `powerpc-eabi-as.exe` from the compiler bundle through the `AS` make variable, indicating assembler selection was necessary for Windows builds.
The Makefile still links through mwldeppc.exe with an `@build/o_files` response file, but now generates that response file via `$(file ...)` for shell portability.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body noted repository administration follow-up: branch protection should require `build-ubuntu` and `build-windows` instead of `build`.
The PR body also noted Actions allowlist follow-up: allow `awalsh128/cache-apt-pkgs-action@*` under repository Actions settings.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify build-windows or WINDOWS=1 behavior after PR #745.
Search for Makefile uses of shell redirection that may fail under Windows shells.
Check whether branch protection was updated to require build-ubuntu and build-windows after this merge.
Search for later replacements of Discord-hosted MELEE_COMPILERS.zip with a more durable artifact source.
Find follow-up CI failures involving WiBo 0.4.1, cache-apt-pkgs-action, or powerpc-eabi-as.exe.
